description:
You are a localization engineer who has just received a new English string that must be shipped in the next release:  

• English key: settings  
• English value: Settings  
• Spanish value: Configuración  

The project is already checked-out in /home/user/project-l18n/ and uses a classic “dotenv” file for run-time variables together with language-specific JSON translation catalogs.

Initial state that already exists when the task starts:
1. /home/user/project-l18n/.env  
   (contains three lines, all without trailing spaces or blank lines)
        API_URL=https://api.example.com  
        FEATURE_X=true  
        MAX_RETRIES=5  

2. /home/user/project-l18n/translations/messages.en.json  
   (a valid single-line JSON object, UTF-8 encoded)  
        {"hello":"Hello","bye":"Good-bye"}

3. /home/user/project-l18n/translations/messages.es.json  
   (a valid single-line JSON object, UTF-8 encoded)  
        {"hello":"Hola","bye":"Adiós"}

Task you must accomplish from the terminal (no GUI tools):
A. Add the new run-time environment variable NEW_STRING with the value Settings as the last line of /home/user/project-l18n/.env.  
   • Preserve all existing lines exactly.  
   • Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline (\n).

B. Insert the new translation pair into the Spanish catalog file so that messages.es.json becomes a valid single-line JSON object containing the new key-value pair  
        "settings":"Configuración"  
   while keeping all pre-existing pairs. Keys do not need to be ordered.

C. After finishing A and B, create a plain-text log file /home/user/update_log.txt with exactly two lines (terminate the second line with a single newline). The content must be precisely:

        VAR_ADDED: NEW_STRING=Settings
        TRANSLATION_ADDED: settings=Configuración

No other text, spacing, or blank lines are allowed in the log file.

Important constraints that the automated checker will verify:
• /home/user/project-l18n/.env must now have four lines, in the original order plus the newly appended line.  
• Both JSON catalog files must remain valid single-line JSON; only messages.es.json should change.  
• /home/user/update_log.txt must exist with exactly the two lines shown above.  
• You may accomplish the entire task with a single well-crafted shell command if you wish, but multiple commands are also acceptable; the final state is what matters.

Do not output code examples here—use the terminal inside the task environment to carry out the work. When everything is correct, no further output is required.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent begins:

1. Path: /home/user/project-l18n/.env  
   Contents (with trailing newline after the last line):
API_URL=https://api.example.com
FEATURE_X=true
MAX_RETRIES=5

2. Path: /home/user/project-l18n/translations/messages.en.json  
   Contents (single line, trailing newline):
{"hello":"Hello","bye":"Good-bye"}

3. Path: /home/user/project-l18n/translations/messages.es.json  
   Contents (single line, trailing newline):
{"hello":"Hola","bye":"Adiós"}

Expected final state for verification:

1. /home/user/project-l18n/.env (four lines, trailing newline):
API_URL=https://api.example.com
FEATURE_X=true
MAX_RETRIES=5
NEW_STRING=Settings

2. /home/user/project-l18n/translations/messages.en.json (unchanged):
{"hello":"Hello","bye":"Good-bye"}

3. /home/user/project-l18n/translations/messages.es.json (single line with new pair, key order not enforced but file must parse to following mapping):
{
 "hello": "Hola",
 "bye": "Adiós",
 "settings": "Configuración"
}
The checker will parse JSON and confirm the three exact key-value pairs, case-sensitive.

4. /home/user/update_log.txt (exact content with trailing newline):
VAR_ADDED: NEW_STRING=Settings
TRANSLATION_ADDED: settings=Configuración

Permissions: all files should be owned by user with default umask; no executable bits required.
